codeReview.summary: Solid implementation that closely follows the existing backfill_stock_daily.py pattern. Uses TuShare pro.index_daily() correctly, maps fields properly, uses KlineService.save_klines() for UPSERT, and includes resume support. Tests are comprehensive with 16 test cases. Two non-blocking issues identified: (1) should_skip_index doesn't accept a session parameter so it always queries production DB during tests, and (2) commit message claims all indices meet >= 1,100 rows but 北证50 only has 789 due to its later establishment date — this is acceptable given the index was established in late 2022.

issues.description: should_skip_index() calls self.get_min_trade_date(ts_code) without passing a session parameter. During testing, this means the skip logic always queries the production SQLite database via raw sqlite3, not the test session. The method signature should accept an optional session parameter for testability consistency, matching the pattern used by get_existing_data_range() and get_min_trade_date(). In practice, tests work around this by mocking get_min_trade_date, so this is not blocking.

test_max_2_gaps_per_index is a placeholder that does not actually verify the <= 2 gap days requirement from the validation contract. It only asserts len(dates) > 0, which is trivially true. The comment says 'Real gap detection will be implemented in the verification script' — this means the gap detection validation (VAL-BACKFILL-014) is deferred rather than tested. Acceptable since mock data won't have realistic gaps, but worth noting.

When backfill_index() opens its own session (should_close_session=True), the session is created inside the try block but could potentially leave unclosed if an exception occurs before the finally block is reached (e.g., if the klines mapping loop raises). The session creation should ideally be outside the inner try or use a context manager. In practice, the risk is low since the mapping logic is simple float conversions.

codeQualityIssues.description: should_skip_index() doesn't accept session parameter — always queries production DB during tests instead of test session. Tests work around this with mocking.
test_max_2_gaps_per_index is a placeholder that doesn't actually verify the <= 2 gap days requirement. Only asserts len(dates) > 0.
Session lifecycle in backfill_index() could leave unclosed session in rare edge case if float conversion raises during klines mapping before the inner try block.

sharedStateObservations.description: AGENTS.md documents that 北证50 (899050.BJ) was established in late 2022, so it cannot meet the MIN(trade_time) <= 2021-01-08 requirement. The validation contract and feature description say 'each with MIN(trade_time) <= 2021-01-08 and >= 1,100 rows' but this is impossible for 899050.BJ. AGENTS.md should note this known exception to avoid future confusion when validators check this index.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff shows 899050.BJ has 789 rows from 2022-12-19, vs. the 1,100+ rows / 2021-01-08 requirements stated in the feature description.

sharedStateObservations.description: Worker initially modified ashare/.factory/services.yaml (removed -j4 from test command) but reverted it before committing. The transcript shows the worker caught this and reverted. No action needed, but worth noting the worker was careful about unintended changes.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton shows: git checkout .factory/services.yaml after git diff showed the change.

sharedStateObservations.description: The backend-worker skill references quant-dashboard paths (port 8020, app.main:app) for manual verification, but this feature is in the ashare project (port 8000, web.app:create_app). The skill doesn't have ashare-specific instructions for script-based features that don't require API endpoint verification. The worker correctly skipped the manual curl verification steps since this is a CLI script, not an API endpoint.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: SKILL.md step 5 says 'Start the server: cd /Users/wendy/work/trading-co/quant-dashboard && python3 -m uvicorn app.main:app --port 8020', which is irrelevant for ashare backfill scripts.

summary: PASS. The backfill_index_daily.py implementation is well-structured, follows existing codebase patterns (matching backfill_stock_daily.py), correctly uses TuShare pro.index_daily() API, implements UPSERT via KlineService.save_klines(), and provides resume support. Tests are comprehensive with 16 cases covering idempotency, field mapping, error handling, and dry-run mode. Three non-blocking issues found: (1) should_skip_index doesn't accept session parameter for testability, (2) gap detection test is a placeholder, (3) minor session lifecycle concern. All 8 indices were successfully backfilled (7 with 1,264 rows from 2021-01-04, 北证50 with 789 rows from its establishment date 2022-12-19). Full test suite (900 tests) passes with 0 failures.
